title: santolina chamaecyparissus var. rigidula
intro: Santolina chamaecyparissus var. rigidula, also known as Greek grey santolina, is a shrubby, perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its silvery-gray foliage and yellow or white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial, antifungal, and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Santolina chamaecyparissus var. rigidula include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system, making it a popular natural remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of Santolina chamaecyparissus var. rigidula include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
